articles.title: Prohibited AI practices
articles.summary: Article 5 prohibits AI systems that pose unacceptable risks to fundamental rights and safety. These practices are banned outright and cannot be made compliant — systems falling under these categories must be redesigned or discontinued before the August 2026 enforcement date.
requirements: Prohibit AI systems that deploy subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness to materially distort their behaviour causing harm
Prohibit AI systems that exploit vulnerabilities of specific groups (age, disability) to distort behaviour causing harm
Prohibit AI systems used by public authorities for social scoring leading to detrimental treatment
Prohibit real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement (with narrow exceptions)

checklist.item: Audit all AI systems to verify none engage in subliminal or manipulative behavioral distortion
Confirm no AI system exploits vulnerabilities of protected groups to cause harm
Confirm no AI system used for public social scoring leading to detrimental or unfavorable treatment
Confirm no real-time remote biometric identification deployed in public spaces (or document applicable law enforcement exception with prior authorisation)
Document decision to discontinue or redesign any system that cannot comply

articles.title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
articles.summary: Article 6 establishes the classification criteria for high-risk AI systems. An AI system is high-risk if it falls within Annex III or if it constitutes a safety component of a product covered by EU harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I. Providers must perform this classification before placing the system on the market.
requirements: Determine whether the AI system is a safety component of a product covered by Annex I Union harmonisation legislation
Determine whether the AI system falls within one of the eight categories listed in Annex III
Document the classification outcome and reasoning in the technical documentation
Apply all Chapter III Section 2 obligations if the system is classified as high-risk

checklist.item: Review Annex I Union harmonisation legislation list and assess whether system is a safety component
Review Annex III eight high-risk categories and assess applicability to the system
Document classification decision with legal and factual justification
If not high-risk, document the self-assessment reasoning for the classification
Re-assess classification upon any significant modification to the AI system

articles.title: Risk management system
articles.summary: Article 9 requires providers of high-risk AI systems to establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system throughout the entire lifecycle. The system must follow a continuous iterative process to identify, analyse, evaluate and mitigate reasonably foreseeable risks, including those arising from misuse.
requirements: Establish and document a continuous risk management process covering the full AI system lifecycle
Identify and analyse known and reasonably foreseeable risks to health, safety and fundamental rights
Estimate and evaluate risks that may emerge from intended use and reasonably foreseeable misuse
Adopt suitable risk mitigation and control measures giving precedence to design-and-development measures
Test the AI system to ensure it consistently performs as intended and risks are effectively controlled
Document residual risks and ensure deployers are informed of them via instructions for use
Review and update the risk management system post-market

checklist.item: Establish documented risk management process covering full lifecycle
Identify and document all known risks to health, safety and fundamental rights
Analyse risks from intended use and reasonably foreseeable misuse scenarios
Define and implement mitigation and control measures, prioritising design-stage measures
Document residual risks after mitigation and communicate them to deployers
Conduct and document pre-market validation testing
Establish review and update schedule for post-market risk monitoring

articles.title: Data and data governance
articles.summary: Article 10 sets requirements for training, validation and testing data used in high-risk AI systems. Data must be subject to governance practices ensuring relevance, representativeness and freedom from errors. Providers must document data sources, preprocessing steps, and bias assessment processes.
requirements: Apply data governance and management practices to training, validation and testing datasets
Ensure training data is relevant, representative, accurate and complete for the intended purpose
Identify, document and address potential biases that could affect fundamental rights or lead to discrimination
Document data acquisition methods, preprocessing, labelling and data cleaning operations
Ensure data protection measures when personal data is processed, including data minimisation
Assess whether training data adequately covers the population and use-case distribution the system will encounter

checklist.item: Document all training, validation and testing data sources with provenance
Define and enforce data quality criteria (accuracy, completeness, representativeness)
Document data preprocessing steps including labelling, cleaning, and enrichment
Conduct and document bias assessment on training and validation datasets
Assess representativeness of data across affected population segments
Establish and document data retention and deletion policies
Document data protection measures where personal data is processed

articles.title: Technical documentation
articles.summary: Article 11 requires providers of high-risk AI systems to draw up technical documentation before placing the system on the market. The documentation must be kept up to date and made available to national competent authorities and notified bodies on request. Its content is specified in Annex IV.
requirements: Prepare technical documentation in accordance with Annex IV before placing the system on the market
Keep technical documentation up to date throughout the system lifecycle
Include a general description, system architecture, training data description, and performance metrics
Document intended purpose, limitations and foreseeable misuse
Document all changes made to the system throughout its lifecycle
Make technical documentation available to competent authorities and notified bodies upon request

checklist.item: Draft technical documentation covering all Annex IV sections before market placement
Document general system description including intended purpose and foreseeable misuse
Document system architecture including components, inputs, outputs and interfaces
Document training data methodology and datasets used
Document performance metrics and validation results
Document known limitations and conditions under which system should not be used
Maintain a changes log for all significant modifications post-deployment

articles.title: Record-keeping
articles.summary: Article 12 requires high-risk AI systems to automatically generate event logs enabling traceability of the system's operation throughout its lifecycle. Logging must be enabled by design and must allow auditing of decisions made with or by the system, particularly in cases affecting individuals.
requirements: Enable automatic logging of events relevant to identifying risks and assessing conformity
Ensure logs capture system inputs, outputs, and decision points enabling full traceability
Retain logs for a period appropriate to the intended purpose and applicable law (minimum: operator duration or 6 months)
Ensure logs are protected against tampering and unauthorised access
Deployers must retain logs under their control for the applicable retention period
Logs must enable audit of decisions affecting individuals and post-incident review

checklist.item: Implement automatic event logging capturing inputs, outputs and decision points
Define log format ensuring structured, machine-readable output
Establish log retention schedule compliant with sector-specific requirements (minimum 6 months)
Implement access controls to protect logs against tampering and unauthorised access
Test logging system to confirm immutability and completeness of records
Document logging configuration in technical documentation and instructions for use

articles.title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
articles.summary: Article 13 requires high-risk AI systems to be sufficiently transparent that deployers can interpret the system's output and use it appropriately. Providers must supply instructions for use including the system's capabilities, limitations, performance metrics, and human oversight requirements.
requirements: Ensure the AI system provides sufficient transparency for deployers to interpret outputs and use the system appropriately
Supply instructions for use covering system identity, capabilities, intended purpose and limitations
Include information on the level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity in the instructions
Describe human oversight measures including how to interpret and override system outputs
Provide information on foreseeable misuse, residual risks and maintenance requirements
Instructions must be in a language understood by deployers and presented in machine-readable format where applicable

checklist.item: Draft instructions for use covering all Article 13(3) required elements
Describe system capabilities, intended purpose and validated use cases
Document known limitations, contraindications and conditions for non-use
Include human oversight instructions: how to interpret, validate and override outputs
Include accuracy and performance metrics at system level and across relevant subgroups
Provide maintenance and update instructions for deployers

articles.title: Human oversight
articles.summary: Article 14 requires high-risk AI systems to be designed and developed in a way that enables effective human oversight during the period of use. Oversight measures must allow natural persons to monitor system operation, understand outputs, and intervene or override as needed.
requirements: Design the AI system to allow effective oversight by natural persons during the period of use
Enable oversight persons to fully understand the capabilities and limitations of the AI system
Enable oversight persons to detect and address automation bias and over-reliance
Provide ability to disregard, override or reverse outputs of the AI system
Enable intervention or interruption of the system via a 'stop' mechanism if the system poses risks
Ensure oversight persons are identified, assigned and appropriately trained before deployment
Deployers must assign oversight responsibilities to competent natural persons

checklist.item: Identify and document designated human oversight roles with responsibilities
Implement mechanism to override or disregard AI system outputs at any time
Implement system interruption/stop mechanism for emergency situations
Document and test monitoring procedures including detection of automation bias
Develop and deliver training for oversight-responsible persons
Define escalation pathways when oversight persons detect anomalies or risks

articles.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
articles.summary: Article 15 requires high-risk AI systems to achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity, and to perform consistently throughout their lifecycle. Providers must implement technical measures to protect against adversarial attacks, data poisoning, and model exploitation.
requirements: Achieve and maintain declared accuracy levels throughout the system lifecycle
Implement measures to ensure resilience against attempts to alter system use or performance through adversarial techniques
Protect against model attacks including data poisoning, adversarial examples and model inversion
Define and implement fallback behaviour when system encounters inputs outside its intended scope
Ensure the system is resilient against errors, faults and inconsistencies in inputs
Implement cybersecurity measures proportionate to the risks and state of the art
Establish update and maintenance processes to preserve accuracy, robustness and security post-deployment

checklist.item: Define target accuracy metrics and acceptable thresholds per use case and subgroup
Conduct robustness testing including edge cases, out-of-distribution inputs and stress tests
Conduct adversarial testing and document protections against adversarial attacks
Define and test fallback behaviour for inputs outside intended operating scope
Implement and document cybersecurity measures (access controls, encryption, secure deployment)
Establish update and patching procedures to maintain security and accuracy post-market

articles.title: Quality management system
articles.summary: Article 17 requires providers of high-risk AI systems to put in place a quality management system (QMS) ensuring compliance with the EU AI Act. The QMS must be documented, maintained and cover the full development and deployment lifecycle, including strategies, processes, procedures, roles and responsibilities.
requirements: Establish and document a quality management system covering compliance with the EU AI Act
Define compliance strategy and procedures for design, development, testing, and post-market monitoring
Document roles, responsibilities and authority structures for compliance activities
Allocate adequate technical and human resources for quality management activities
Implement change management procedures for modifications to the AI system
Conduct internal audits at planned intervals to verify compliance
Implement corrective action procedures when non-conformities are identified

checklist.item: Establish and document the quality management system with defined scope
Document compliance strategy covering all applicable EU AI Act obligations
Define and assign roles and responsibilities for compliance activities
Document resource allocation for QMS activities (personnel, tools, budget)
Establish and follow internal audit schedule with documented outcomes
Implement and document corrective action procedure for non-conformities
Implement change management process for significant system modifications

articles.title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
articles.summary: Article 25 sets out specific obligations for deployers of high-risk AI systems. Deployers must use systems in accordance with instructions, assign human oversight, implement input data governance, monitor operation, and report serious incidents to providers and national authorities.
requirements: Use the AI system in accordance with the provider's instructions for use
Assign human oversight to natural persons with necessary competence, authority and resources
Ensure input data is relevant and sufficiently representative for the intended purpose
Monitor the operation of the AI system and report anomalies, malfunctions or serious incidents
Report serious incidents to the provider and national competent authorities
Conduct a fundamental rights impact assessment where required (public bodies and certain private entities)

checklist.item: Document the specific use case and verify it aligns with provider's intended purpose
Assign and document natural person(s) responsible for human oversight with defined authority
Implement and document input data governance including quality and representativeness checks
Establish operational monitoring procedure and document monitoring outputs
Establish incident reporting procedure for serious incidents to provider and authorities
Assess and document whether a fundamental rights impact assessment is required

articles.title: Transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems
articles.summary: Article 50 establishes transparency obligations for AI systems that interact with natural persons or generate synthetic content. It requires disclosure that users are interacting with an AI, and that AI-generated content (audio, video, images, text) is labelled as such. Applies to GPAI models and specific limited-risk systems.
requirements: Disclose to users that they are interacting with an AI system, unless it is obvious from context
Label AI-generated audio, video, image and text content so it is detectable as AI-generated
Providers of GPAI models generating synthetic content must implement technical marking (watermarking) where technically feasible
Deployers using systems to generate or manipulate content must label that content as AI-generated
Disclosure must be made at the latest at the time of first interaction or before content is released

checklist.item: Implement user-facing disclosure that the system is an AI at point of first interaction
Implement labelling or watermarking for AI-generated audio, image, video and text content
For GPAI models: implement technical machine-readable content marking where feasible
Document the disclosure and labelling mechanism and test its effectiveness
Verify disclosure is presented in plain language understandable by target users

articles.title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems (chatbots, deepfakes)
articles.summary: Article 52 addresses transparency obligations for limited-risk AI systems, specifically chatbots and systems generating deepfakes. Users must be informed they are interacting with an AI, and deepfake content must be clearly labelled. These obligations apply independently of any high-risk classification.
requirements: Ensure AI chatbots and conversational agents inform users they are interacting with an AI unless obvious from context
Clearly label AI-generated or manipulated images, video and audio (deepfakes) as artificially generated
Disclosure must be given at the start of the first interaction or before content is presented
Exceptions apply where prior explicit consent is given or where the content serves authorised law enforcement purposes

checklist.item: Implement AI disclosure notice at the start of chatbot or conversational AI interactions
Implement visible and clear labelling on all deepfake or AI-manipulated audio/video/image content
Verify disclosure is prominent and cannot be easily missed or dismissed
Document any consent or exception conditions relied upon for reduced disclosure
Provide contact information enabling users to query AI-generated content status

articles.title: High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)
articles.summary: Annex III lists the eight categories of AI systems that are classified as high-risk under Article 6(2). Providers must assess whether their system falls into one of these categories as part of the Article 6 classification process. The list may be amended by the Commission via delegated acts.
requirements: Category 1 — Biometrics: remote biometric identification systems and biometric categorisation systems
Category 2 — Critical infrastructure: safety components in management of critical digital, road, rail, water, energy infrastructure
Category 3 — Education and vocational training: systems for access, admission and assessment determining educational outcomes
Category 4 — Employment and workers management: recruitment, selection, performance evaluation, promotion and termination decisions
Category 5 — Essential private and public services: creditworthiness assessment, life/health insurance risk assessment, emergency service dispatch, social benefit eligibility
Category 6 — Law enforcement: risk assessment of individuals for criminal offences, polygraphs, crime analytics profiling, evidence evaluation
Category 7 — Migration, asylum and border control: risk assessment of migrants, examination of applications, border surveillance
Category 8 — Administration of justice and democratic processes: AI assisting courts in research and decisions affecting legal rights

checklist.item: Assess system against Category 1 (Biometrics) — document outcome
Assess system against Category 2 (Critical Infrastructure) — document outcome
Assess system against Category 3 (Education and Vocational Training) — document outcome
Assess system against Category 4 (Employment and Workers Management) — document outcome
Assess system against Category 5 (Essential Private and Public Services) — document outcome
Assess system against Category 6 (Law Enforcement) — document outcome
Assess system against Category 7 (Migration, Asylum, Border Control) — document outcome
Assess system against Category 8 (Administration of Justice) — document outcome

articles.title: Technical documentation referred to in Article 11(1)
articles.summary: Annex IV specifies the mandatory content for technical documentation required under Article 11. The documentation must be compiled before market placement and kept up to date. It is the primary evidence base for conformity assessment and must be made available to competent authorities and notified bodies on request.
requirements: Section 1 — General description of the AI system: intended purpose, system version, hardware/software requirements, instructions for use
Section 2 — Description of elements and development process: design, development and training methods, architecture, data used, training and testing methodologies

Section 3 — Detailed information on monitoring, functioning and control: capabilities and limitations, accuracy levels, known biases, foreseeable unintended outcomes and circumstances in which the system may fail
Section 4 — Description of appropriateness of performance metrics: metrics used to measure accuracy, robustness, safety and non-discrimination, with justification for chosen metrics
Section 5 — Description of known and foreseeable circumstances leading to risks: including risks to health, safety and fundamental rights, with mitigating measures
Section 6 — Description of changes made throughout the lifecycle: post-deployment changes, modifications, updates and their impact on conformity
Section 7 — List of harmonised standards applied: reference to harmonised standards or common specifications applied in whole or in part, and solutions adopted to satisfy requirements
Section 8 — Copy of the EU declaration of conformity referred to in Article 47

checklist.item: Draft Section 1: General description including intended purpose and system versions
Draft Section 2: Development process description including architecture and training methodology
Draft Section 3: Monitoring, functioning and control with capabilities and known limitations
Draft Section 4: Performance metrics with justification for chosen measurement approach
Draft Section 5: Risk circumstances including foreseeable failure modes and mitigations
Draft Section 6: Lifecycle changes log covering all post-deployment modifications
Draft Section 7: List of harmonised standards and common specifications applied
Attach or reference Section 8: EU declaration of conformity (Article 47)
